markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Find possible conflicting claims by symbolic keys, aliases, source proximity, and embedding/vector-like evidence where available. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: ConflictCandidateSearch leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ConflictCandidateSearch is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 91c204e6; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-121 requires ConflictCandidateSearch: find possible conflicting claims by symbolic keys/aliases/source-proximity (embedding evidence "where available"), implemented Handshake-native on PostgreSQL/EventLedger. The code delivers a real, deterministic, idempotent symbolic candidate search over knowledge_memory_facts backed by the committed knowledge_claims/knowledge_claim_conflicts substrate, proven against real managed PostgreSQL. It aligns with current Master Spec 2.3.13.11.
findings: find_fact_conflict_candidates (src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/knowledge_memory.rs:859-907) is the MT-121 deliverable: a real PostgreSQL self-join over knowledge_memory_facts finding pairs sharing (subject_entity_id, predicate_key) but with COALESCE(object_entity_id,object_literal) IS DISTINCT FROM. Ordered a.fact_id<b.fact_id so each unordered pair appears once (deterministic/idempotent). This is the symbolic-key conflict candidate search the MT requires.

Real Handshake-owned resource touch: candidates feed run_symbolic_conflict_detection (conflict.rs:59-106) which records committed knowledge_claim_conflicts via db.record_knowledge_claim_conflict (moving both backing claims to 'conflicted'), then files a typed detection-job. Migration 0242 (knowledge_memory_conflict_detection_jobs/findings, resolution_jobs) FKs into kernel_event_ledger(event_id) and knowledge_claim_conflicts(conflict_id) — durable PG/EventLedger authority, no SQLite/external-engine path (DEC-008 honored).

Proof is real, not implementer-authored mock: tests/knowledge_memory_conflict_tests.rs:64-131 build two facts disagreeing on object on a real managed-PG fixture, assert exactly one candidate, assert detection records a conflict + both claims become Conflicted, and assert re-run yields 0 new conflicts (idempotent). The fixture harness knowledge_pg_support.rs is owned by an earlier MT (commit 3b566587), is the shared managed-PostgreSQL discovery path, and SKIPs loudly when PG is absent rather than substituting a fake (knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs:9, conflict_tests.rs:66).

Sub-rule 1 clean: grep of conflict.rs + knowledge_memory.rs + conflict_tests.rs found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/"deferred"/"pending MT-". The only forward-looking note (semantic/embedding candidates as a future extension, conflict.rs:20-21, knowledge_memory.rs:855-857) is exactly what the MT scope defers via "embedding/vector-like evidence where available" — the required symbolic search is fully built, not stubbed.

Spec alignment: current Master Spec 02-system-architecture.md 2.3.13.11 (v02.194, line 2704) requires KnowledgeClaim to carry lifecycle including 'conflicted', conflict refs, and resolution receipts on PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority; the impl drives exactly that flow and reuses the claim conflict substrate rather than duplicating it.

Aside (non-blocking, governance): MT contract lifecycle.claimed_by/completed_by are null and packet cites v02.192 anchors that resolve to identical v02.194 section numbers — expected paperwork drift during governance wind-down, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
